etymology: Angel = divine messenger; Archer = one who aims
wordplay_note: A messenger aiming at truth; also recalls the archer-god Apollo
card_description: Angel Archer (meaning "Angel = divine messenger; Archer = one who aims") is a character in Philip K. Dick's *The Transmigration of Timothy Archer*. Narrator and daughter-in-law of Bishop Archer who is the novel's rational center. Name note: A messenger aiming at truth; also recalls the archer-god Apollo. The witness-narrator who survives while the seekers around her self-destruct. First appears in *The Transmigration of Timothy Archer*.

reference.brief: Narrator and daughter-in-law of Bishop Archer who is the novel's rational center
reference.etymology: Angel = divine messenger; Archer = one who aims
